rulings.Case Title: SALMA KHAN MD VS. HEALTH SYSTEMS DEPARTMENT, SAN MATEO COUNTY, et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Ptlf'S Deposition Testimony And Accompanying Request For Production Of Documents And Things; And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Hearing set for Monday, September 23, 2013, Line 2: Defendants COUNTY OF SAN MATEO'S and CELIA MORENO, M.D.'S Motion to Compel Plaintiff's Deposition Testimony and Accompanying Request for Production of Documents and Things; and for Sanctions Part 1 of 2: Pro Tem Judge Adrian Colley, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: In this action brought by plaintiff pro per for various employment causes of action arising from her 2009 termination as a psychiatrist by San Mateo county, defendants, including, inter alia, Health Systems Department, San Mateo Co., have made a Second Motion to Compel Plaintiff's Deposition and Accompanying Request for Production of Documents and Things and for Sanctions. Defendants' motion is granted in the following respects: 1. Plaintiff and counsel for defendants shall meet and confer on these discovery matters by no later than October 2, 2013. 2. Plaintiff shall present herself for a second deposition by defendants by no later than October 18, 2013, to answer questions with respect to her supplemental answers to written discovery, her alleged wage losses, physical injuries, and general damages, and by that date she also shall have produced any documents in response to the demand for the production of documents served with the deposition notice. 3. In the event that plaintiff does not so timely present herself for the aforesaid deposition and timely and fully produce the aforesaid documents required by the above mentioned demand for the production of documents, then A. Plaintiff will be prevented from alleging or bringing any evidence with respect to the matters set forth in defendants' notice of motion for the subject motion, and B. Defendants may serve subpoenas for documents and supplemental subpoenas on Plaintiff's CPA, medical providers and employers, and [Continued to Part 2 of 2] =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: ATLANTIC CREDIT & FINANCE, INC. ASSIGNEE OF VS. THERESA M ROGERS
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of And Mtn For An Assignment Order And Accounting Of Funds
rulings.Rulings: Hearing set for Monday, September 23, 2013, Line 3: Plaintiff ATLANTIC CREDIT & FINANCE, INC. ASSIGNEE OF HOUSEHOLD'S Motion for an Assignment Order and Accounting of Funds Pro Tem Judge Adrian Colley, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff's motion for an assignment order and accounting of funds is granted. It should be noted that the motion is unopposed and the moving papers appear to adequately support the motion. From plaintiff's papers it appears that this is a garden variety request for an assignment order under CA law of defendant's receipts from her income as an independent contractor. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to adrianacolley@aol.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO,ACTING VS. CURTIS LIND et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn For Terminating Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Hearing set for Monday, September 23, 2013, Line 4: Plaintiff CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, ACTING BY AND THROUGH SAN FRANCISCO PORT COMMISSION'S Motion for Terminating Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Adrian Colley, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff's motion for terminating and monetary sanctions in the amount of $2040.00 is granted. The motion is unopposed. It should be noted that the papers submitted by the plaintiff demonstrate good cause for the granting of the motion. The City (plaintiff) previously obtained in August, 2013, an unopposed order to compel discovery from defendant with respect to earlier discovery requests from plaintiff that defendant had completely disregarded. Counsel for defendant at that time advised plaintiff that he had not been able to contact defendant to advise him of that order. The instant motion of plaintiff for the terminating and monetary sanctions is the result of defendant's complete disregard for, and non compliance with, the court's August order to compel discovery by defendant. Plaintiff's papers in support of its motion are persuasive. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to adrianacolley@aol.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: URS CORPORATION DBA URS CORPORATION AMERICAS, A VS. UNIVERSAL MOLDING EXTRUSION COMPANY, INC. DBA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Privilege Log And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Hearing set for Monday, September 23, 2013, Line 6: Plaintiff URS CORPORATION DBA URS CORPORATION AMERICAS' Motion to Privilege Log and for Sanctions PART 1 OF 2: Pro Tem Judge Adrian Colley, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff's motion for an order compelling defendant to serve by October 1, 2013 a further privilege log responsive to defendant's request for production served on plaintiff March 29, 2013, is granted. Plaintiff's further motion for an order for monetary sanctions to be imposed on defendant is denied. This is an action by plaintiff to recover unpaid compensation allegedly due it by defendant pursuant to a master services agreement between plaintiff and defendant pursuant to which plaintiff has provided services and goods and materials to defendant for which plaintiff has not been compensated as required by the agreement. A primary defense raised by defendant is that John Wall, the defendant's employee who negotiated and executed the work orders and the master service agreement, lacked the authority to do so. In discovery, plaintiff requested documents related to defendant's employment of John Wall. In response, defendant asserted third party privilege and refused to produce any responsive documents. For its part, plaintiff demanded a privilege log, but in providing a log defendant furnished no detail regarding the specific documents withheld and the basis for the assertion of privilege. Plaintiff has requested a more specific privilege log. Defendant however has declined to do so on the ground that privacy is not a privilege for which the statutory privilege log requirement applies. The agreement in section 18.1 provides in pertinent part that each of the parties "represent and warrant that this agreement has been duly authorized, executed and delivered and constitutes its binding agreement enforceable against it." [CONTINUED TO PART 2 OF 2] =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: URS CORPORATION DBA URS CORPORATION AMERICAS, A VS. UNIVERSAL MOLDING EXTRUSION COMPANY, INC. DBA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Urs Corporation Dba Urs Corporation Americas' Motion To Privilege Log And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Hearing set for Monday, September 23, 2013, Line 6: Plaintiff URS CORPORATION DBA URS CORPORATION AMERICAS' Motion to Privilege Log and for Sanctions PART 2 OF 2: Plaintiff's reply brief, filed September 16, 2013, citing the Court of Appeal decision in Alch v. Super. Ct (2008), 165 Cal. App. 4th 1412, 1432-1433, and other decisions cited in its brief, makes the point that plaintiff "is not seeking John Wall's sensitive or confidential information.... Indeed, counsel for [plaintiff]... stated that it is looking for information relevant to the authority/agency defence that UMEX has raised, such as information regarding Mr. Wall's position, title, job description, and other employment related information.... Here, [plaintiff} has separated the sensitive private information that it cannot seek from the non-sensitive information that it does seek, just as in Alch ....Here, UMEX and URS contracted for environmental consulting services. John Wall's role in contracting for these and other types of services in furtherance of UMEX's business is relevant to this action, specifically as to whether Mr. Wall had authority to enter into the master agreement and work orders in dispute."... It would appear that the defendant's assertion of Mr. Wall's lack of authority as a defense to plaintiff's action to recover compensation under the agreement makes the arguments advanced by plaintiff cited above a justifiable and supportable basis for the granting of plaintiff's motion in this instance. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to adrianacolley@aol.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: NICHOLAS BLASGEN VS. REFRACTIVE DIALER, LLC*****CASE TRANSFERRED TO SACRAMENTO COUNTY*****
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Change Venue
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Monday, September 23, 2013, Line 15, DEFENDANT REFRACTIVE DIALER, LLC Mtn To Change Venue Granted. Cal. Corp. Code §177 provides that the proper venue for involuntary dissolution proceedings is the county where the corporation's principal office is located. The current designation filed with the Secretary of State conclusively fixes the corporation's principal place of business for purposes of venue. (Rosas v. Superior Court (1994) 25 Cal.App.4th 671, 677.) A company may not defeat venue based on that designation by showing that its principal place of business is located elsewhere. (Id.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Monday, September 23, 2013, Line 16, DEFENDANT RANDALL WOLF Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Service Of Summons Or Stay Or Dismiss Action Granted. Plaintiffs have not shown by a preponderance of the evidence that Defendant has sufficient minimum contacts with California to support personal jurisdiction. Plaintiff has only showed a one-time purchase of a magic show and three trips to California for training purposes. This is a one-time transaction, and not on going business dealings. rulings.Case Title: MARK BELANGER VS. WINE.COM et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Monday, September 23, 2013, Line 21, DEFENDANT WINE.COM MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT Defendant's Motion to Strike is denied. Plaintiff has pled specific facts alleging that an officer of the corporation directed Plaintiff to commit fraud, and after Plaintiff refused to do so, he was terminated. These facts are sufficient to meet the standard of "oppression, fraud, or malice" set forth in Cal. Civ. Code § 3294(b). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: MARK BELANGER VS. WINE.COM et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Monday, September 23, 2013, Line 21, DEFENDANT WINE.COM DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT Defendant Wine.com's Demurrer to the Complaint is sustained in part and overruled in part. The demurrer is sustained with 20 days leave to amend as to the second and third causes of action for breach of contract and breach of implied covenant of good faith and fair dealing. Plaintiff has not pled facts sufficient to overcome the presumption that the employment was at will. (See Cal. Lab. Code §2922.) Plaintiff has not pled facts sufficient to establish that the parties' conduct revealed a promise not to discharge without good cause or for a certain period of time. (See Foley v. Interactive Data Corp. (1988) 47 Cal.3d 654, 675.) Because Plaintiff did not plead facts sufficient to establish an implied-in-fact contract, no facts have been pled to establish a cause of action for breach of the covenant of good faith and fair dealing, as that cause of action requires the existence of a contract. (Wilson v. 21st Century Ins. Co. (2007) 42 Cal.4th 713, 720; "The law imposes a covenant of good faith and fair dealing in every contract.") The demurrer is overruled as to the fourth cause of action. Plaintiff has pled facts sufficient to establish that Mr. Chow knew the statements were false when made and therefore has pled facts sufficient to establish the malice required to overcome the qualified privilege. (Complaint, 31, "Chow also knew that the statement was false.") Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests.
